selected_successor: NA-0316 -- Metadata Runtime qshield Poll No-Mutation Blocker Resolution

candidate_runtime_surfaces.future_proof: stale, malformed, replayed, and wrong-scope peer or bundle handles reject without accepted-state mutation and without raw handle output

candidate_runtime_surfaces.surface: qshield relay queue and poll handles
candidate_runtime_surfaces.future_proof: poll and receive reject paths preserve the selected no-mutation boundary before decode or padding failure
default padding policy, bucket table, invalid config reject, strip/verify, malformed padded input reject, and no secret output
padded-mode bucket and metadata reporting remain explicit without claiming metadata-free runtime behavior
route-token and contact/device handle proofs avoid raw token output and preserve reject no-mutation

identifier_plan.required_no_mutation_boundary: accepted runtime state and any scoped relay queue state selected by the future lane
identifier_plan.required_no_log_boundary: no raw handle, route token, bearer token, plaintext sentinel, or internal path in stdout, stderr, logs, or artifacts

padding_plan.required_no_mutation_boundary: accepted runtime state and any scoped relay queue state selected by the future lane
padding_plan.required_no_log_boundary: no raw plaintext, plaintext sentinel, unscoped exact sensitive length, or padding sentinel in stdout, stderr, logs, or artifacts

evidence: apps/qshield-cli/src/commands/relay.rs /poll removes queued entries with pop_front before returning them
apps/qshield-cli/src/commands/recv.rs performs padding strip and actor decode after relay poll returns
a malformed padded message can therefore be removed from the remote queue before local reject
